# game

> An ECS game framework for Moku â€” Spark-style public API and memory layout, PixiJS v8 rendering, built on @moku-labs/core, composable with @moku-labs/web, with a first-class MCP plugin exposing the live runtime to agent clients.

`game` is a typed Entity-Component-System runtime composed of twenty-four plugins (sixteen runtime plugins + an additive 8-plugin Layer-2 in-browser editor subsystem, #17â€“#24). It is unpublished
(version 0.0.0) and not a game engine UI or editor â€” it owns the data model (archetype object-SoA
storage), the fixed-timestep frame, and the GPU lifecycle; you write components and systems in
TypeScript. Hot-path frame work bypasses the Moku kernel for performance and is NOT emitted as
kernel events. Systems reach shared services (assets, logging, frame time) through a typed world
**resource registry** â€” the system signature stays `(world, dt)`.

## Core Concepts

- **Three-layer Moku model.** `createCoreConfig` (Layer 1: config + events) â†’ `createCore`
  (Layer 2: framework + the 24 built-in plugins) â†’ `createApp({ pluginConfigs })` (Layer 3: your
  app). Consumers import `createApp` / `createPlugin` from `game` and never import
  `@moku-labs/core` directly. `createApp(...)` returns an App synchronously; `await app.start()`
  boots the Pixi Application and registers DOM listeners. Each plugin's API is reached as
  `app.<plugin>.<method>()` (e.g. `app.ecs`, `app.scheduler`, `app.renderer`, `app.context`).
  **Advanced / headless escape hatches** (also exported from `game`): `createCore(carrier, { plugins })`
  assembles a custom core from a plugin subset (carrier is type-only â€” pass `{ createPlugin }` or
  `coreConfig`); `createCoreConfig(name, { config, plugins })` (re-exported from `@moku-labs/core`)
  builds a bespoke Layer-1 config with its own `Config`/`Events`/core-plugin set. `createApp` stays
  the default â€” these are for tooling, tests, and agent-only hosts.
- **Config composition.** The framework defines no global config fields (`Config = {}`). All
  configuration is per-plugin via `createApp({ pluginConfigs: { <plugin>: { ... } } })`. Core
  plugins `logPlugin` + `envPlugin` from `@moku-labs/common` are registered in `config.ts`, so
  `ctx.log` and `ctx.env` are on every plugin context.
- **ECS data model.** `app.ecs` returns the `World` facade directly (no wrapper). Components are
  registered with `defineComponent(create, opts?)` and are themselves callable: `Position({x,y})`
  produces a `ComponentInit` spawn payload. `opts.name` makes a component discoverable by name via
  the introspection facet. Storage is archetype object-SoA by default (`"archetype"`) with a
  per-component `"sparse"` opt-out for high-churn tags. `Entity` is a generational handle
  (`(generation << 16) | index`), so stale handles are detectably dead via `isAlive`. Typed
  variadic `query(...)` covers arities 1â€“8. Structural mutations during iteration go through a
  deferred command buffer flushed at each stage boundary.
- **ECS introspection facet (read-only, for tooling such as mcp).** `liveEntities()`,
  `entityCount()`, `componentNames()`, `componentsOf(entity)`, and `componentByName(name)` expose
  the live world without touching layout. `componentByName` resolves a component registered with
  `opts.name` to its callable token (widened to `Component<Record<string, unknown>>` so callers
  pass partial values without per-component generics); `undefined` for unknown/anonymous names,
  first-wins on duplicate names.
- **World resources (first-class systems access).** The world owns a typed singleton registry â€”
  `Resource<T>` tokens identify shared services/state systems read through the `world` argument
  with no closure capture. `defineResource(create?)` mints an auto-keyed (`"res:N"`) token and
  registers an optional lazy factory; `setResource`/`getResource`/`resource`/`hasResource`/
  `removeResource` read and write. `resource(token)` asserts presence (THROWS an actionable error
  if unset with no factory); `getResource(token)` returns `T | undefined`. Resource ops are
  **immediate** â€” they bypass the command buffer even mid-iteration, and are NOT counted by
  `maxStructuralOpsWarn`. Framework well-known resources are wired at `app.start()`: `Assets` +
  `GameContext` (context plugin), `Time` (loop plugin).
- **Fixed-timestep loop.** The `loop` plugin owns the frame: a `requestAnimationFrame` loop with a
  fixed-timestep accumulator. Each frame it clamps the real delta (`maxFrameDelta`), runs
  `scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` zero-or-more times (capped at `maxStepsPerFrame`), then calls
  `renderer.render()` once. `step()` advances exactly one deterministic tick + render and now
  RETURNS a `TimeStepResult` (`{ frame, elapsed, dt }`) snapshot of the just-advanced clock
  (`{ 0, 0, 0 }` before start / after stop; widening from `void` is non-breaking). Each fixed step
  it updates the `Time` world resource in place (`{ dt, elapsed, frame }`, seconds; no per-step
  allocation).
- **Scheduler stages.** Five fixed, ordered stages: `input â†’ update â†’ physics â†’ sync â†’ render`.
  Systems are `(world, dt) => void`. The command buffer is flushed between stages. `scheduler` is a
  thin facade that forwards to `world.addSystem` / `world.tick`.
- **Polled input + injection.** DOM listeners mutate a live resource; an `"input"`-stage system
  freezes it into an immutable `InputSnapshot` per frame. Programmatic `keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress`
  inject keys (for agents/tests); each normalizes a small alias set first â€” `"Space"`/`"Spacebar"`
  â†’ `" "`, `"Esc"` â†’ `"Escape"`, all other keys pass through â€” so the snapshot observes the
  canonical `KeyboardEvent.key`.
- **MCP runtime exposure.** The `mcp` plugin attaches a Model Context Protocol server exposing
  read-only + mutating tools and JSON resources (15 tools total). The transport default is
  **environment-aware**: `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun, `["inMemory"]` in a browser (an in-page
  `InMemoryTransport` pair, no socket â€” reach the client side via `app.mcp.clientTransport()`);
  Streamable HTTP is opt-in. The stdio transport is guarded on `process?.stdin` so a browser
  default never crashes. Mutating tools enqueue closures drained on the next `"input"` stage tick
  (frame-safe). Mutations are honest: unknown component/scene names, dead entities, and headless
  renderers return MCP `isError` / `{ changed: false }` rather than optimistic success.
- **Headless renderer.** `renderer.headless` (auto-detected: `true` when `typeof document ===
  "undefined"`, explicitly overridable) skips Pixi/GPU entirely in `onStart` â€” no `Application` is
  created, `state.app` stays `undefined`, `render()`/`getView()`/`getStage()`/`screenshot()`/
  `tree()`/`attachPrimitive()` become safe no-ops/`undefined`/`false` â€” but `Transform` is still
  defined and the `sync` system still registered, so ECS/scene code is identical headless or not.

## Quick Reference

```ts
import { createApp, createPlugin } from "game";

const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: {
    ecs:       { initialCapacity: 1024, maxStructuralOpsWarn: 0 },
    scheduler: { strictStages: true },
    renderer:  { width: 800, height: 600, background: 0x000000, antialias: true, mount: "#game" },
    input:     { target: "window", keyboard: true, pointer: true, preventDefault: false },
    loop:      { fixedDt: 1 / 60, maxFrameDelta: 0.25, maxStepsPerFrame: 5, autoStart: true },
    assets:    { basePath: "assets/", manifest: { ship: "sprites/ship.png" }, throwOnError: true },
    context:   { bindGameContext: true },
    scene:     { despawnOnUnload: true },
    mcp:       { transports: ["stdio"], httpPort: 3333, httpAuth: "none", enableMutations: true }
  }
});
await app.start();          // async â€” boots Pixi Application, attaches DOM listeners, binds resources
// ... use the app ...
await app.stop();           // tears down GPU + listeners
```

### app.ecs â€” the World facade (Complex)

```ts
defineComponent<T>(create: () => T, opts?: { storage?: "archetype" | "sparse"; name?: string }): Component<T>
defineTag(opts?: { storage?: "archetype" | "sparse"; name?: string }): Tag      // storage default "sparse"
// component tokens are callable: Position({ x, y }) => ComponentInit
spawn(...parts: ComponentInit[]): Entity
despawn(entity: Entity): void
isAlive(entity: Entity): boolean
add<T>(entity, component, value?: Partial<T>): void
remove<T>(entity, component): void
has<T>(entity, component): boolean
get<T>(entity, component): T | undefined
set<T>(entity, component, value: Partial<T>): void
query(...components): Query<Values>            // overloads arity 1..8
//   Query: updateEach(cb), count(), first(), [Symbol.iterator]
addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void
tick(dt: number): void                         // runs all stages in order, flushes buffer between
// â”€â”€ Introspection (read-only â€” for tooling such as the mcp plugin) â”€â”€
liveEntities(): readonly Entity[]              // fresh snapshot of all live entity handles
entityCount(): number                          // direct count of live entities
componentNames(): readonly string[]            // names of components defined with opts.name (registration order)
componentsOf(entity): ReadonlyArray<{ name: string; value: unknown }>  // named components on an entity
componentByName(name: string): Component<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined  // name â†’ callable token; first-wins on dup
// â”€â”€ World resources (typed singletons) â€” immediate, never command-buffered â”€â”€
defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>   // mints "res:N" key + optional lazy factory
setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void
getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T | undefined  // lazy-init from factory; undefined if unset+no factory
resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T              // same lazy-init, THROWS if unset + no factory
hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean     // true if a value is set OR a factory exists
removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void     // clears value; factory (if any) re-inits next read
// Resource<T> = { readonly __key: string; readonly __value?: T }  // __value is phantom (type-level only)
```

### app.scheduler (Standard)

```ts
readonly stages: readonly Stage[]              // ["input","update","physics","sync","render"]
addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void   // forwards to world.addSystem
tick(dt: number): void                                // forwards to world.tick
```

### app.renderer (Complex) â€” PixiJS v8 (headless-capable)

```ts
readonly Transform: Component<{ x; y; rotation; scaleX; scaleY }>   // throws if read before start(); defined even when headless
attach(entity: Entity, view: Container): void  // Container/Sprite/Graphics â€” caller owns stage.addChild
attachPrimitive(entity: Entity, spec: PrimitiveSpec): boolean  // builds a Pixi Graphics from plain data, ADDS it to the stage; false when headless/before start
detach(entity: Entity): void                   // destroy()s view, idempotent
render(): void                                 // draws one frame; no-op before start AND when headless
screenshot(): Promise<string | undefined>      // PNG data URL via Pixi extract; undefined when headless/before start
tree(): SceneNode | undefined                  // JSON scene-graph snapshot; SceneNode.type âˆˆ Text|Graphics|Sprite|Container
getView(): HTMLCanvasElement | undefined       // canvas for manual mounting; undefined when headless
getStage(): Container | undefined              // root Pixi stage; undefined when headless
getEntityView(entity: Entity): Container | undefined  // entity's attached view (undefined if none/headless); for view-local state (tint/alpha) the Transform sync doesn't manage â€” e.g. vfx flash
markDirty(entity: Entity): void                // re-sync view next "sync" tick
// PrimitiveStyle = { fill?: number; stroke?: number; strokeWidth?: number; alpha?: number; label?: string }  // hex-int colors, no Pixi types
// PrimitiveSpec = discriminated union on `shape`:
//   { shape:"rect"; width; height } | { shape:"circle"; radius }
//   | { shape:"line"; x2; y2 } | { shape:"polygon"; points: ReadonlyArray<{x;y}> }  (each & PrimitiveStyle)
//   Anchor: rect & circle are CENTERED on the entity's Transform; polygon points are local (origin at Transform); line runs from the origin to (x2,y2).
// Config.headless: boolean â€” @default detectHeadless() (true when typeof document === "undefined").
```

### app.input (Standard) â€” polled + injectable

```ts
snapshot(): InputSnapshot
keyDown(key: string): void    // hold a key (alias-normalized: "Space"/"Spacebar"â†’" ", "Esc"â†’"Escape")
keyUp(key: string): void      // release a key (alias-normalized)
keyPress(key: string): void   // one-frame tap: justPressed + justReleased, never sticks (alias-normalized)
// InputSnapshot:
//   isDown(key: string): boolean
//   justPressed(key: string): boolean         // one frame only
//   justReleased(key: string): boolean         // one frame only
//   readonly pointer: { x: number; y: number; buttons: number }  // buttons: 1=L 2=R 4=M
// Injection is immediate (between frames, NOT command-buffered); the snapshot observes the canonical key.
```

### app.loop (Standard) â€” fixed-timestep rAF + Time resource

```ts
start(): void          // begin/resume rAF loop (no-op if running)
stop(): void           // pause; cancels pending frame
isRunning(): boolean
step(): TimeStepResult // one deterministic fixed tick + render; returns { frame, elapsed, dt }; { 0,0,0 } before start/after stop
readonly time: Resource<TimeState>   // well-known Time token, also exported as Time from loop/resources
// TimeState (all readonly to consumers; loop mutates in place each step â€” no allocation):
//   dt: number        // fixed step length in SECONDS (== config.fixedDt)
//   elapsed: number   // total simulated time in SECONDS (sum of all fixed steps)
//   frame: number     // count of fixed steps since start (not seconds)
// TimeStepResult = Pick<TimeState, "frame" | "elapsed" | "dt">
// read from a system: world.resource(app.loop.time) â†’ { dt, elapsed, frame }
```

### app.assets (Standard) â€” Pixi v8 Assets wrapper

```ts
load(alias: string): Promise<Texture>                                   // emits assets:loaded {kind:"asset"}
loadBundle(bundle: string, entries: Record<string,string>): Promise<Record<string,Texture>> // emits {kind:"bundle"}
get(alias: string): Texture | undefined                                 // cache read, no load
sprite(alias: string): Promise<Sprite>                                  // load-on-miss, build Sprite
isLoaded(alias: string): boolean                                        // session state, not Pixi cache
```

### app.context (Standard) â€” well-known world resources

```ts
readonly assets: Resource<AssetsApi>           // __key "ctx:assets"; ALWAYS bound at onStart
readonly game:   Resource<GameContextValue>    // __key "ctx:game"; bound only when bindGameContext (default true)
// Both tokens are fixed-key module consts (valid before start); onStart binds their VALUES on the world.
// GameContextValue = { readonly log: LogApi; readonly emit: EmitFn<FrameworkEvents>; readonly env: EnvApi }
// read from a system: world.resource(app.context.assets) / world.resource(app.context.game)
```

### app.scene (Standard) â€” named lifecycle + ownership

```ts
define(name: string, definition: SceneDefinition): void
//   SceneDefinition = { setup: (world: World) => void | Promise<void>; bundle?: Record<string,string> }
load(name: string): Promise<void>     // unload current â†’ preload bundle â†’ run setup â†’ emit scene:loaded
unload(): void                        // detach + despawn owned entities (if despawnOnUnload)
currentScene(): string | undefined
sceneNames(): readonly string[]       // registered scene names (registration order); [] before any define
ownedEntities(): readonly Entity[]    // snapshot of current scene's owned entities; [] after unload
```

### app.mcp (Complex) â€” MCP server (env-aware transports)

```ts
isRunning(): boolean
httpEndpoint(): string | undefined    // e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:3333/mcp", or undefined when no "http" transport
toolNames(): readonly string[]
clientTransport(): InMemoryClientTransportLike | undefined  // in-page client paired with the server when "inMemory" is active
// Config.transports: ReadonlyArray<"stdio" | "http" | "inMemory">
//   @default env-aware: ["inMemory"] in a browser, ["stdio"] under Node/Bun. "inMemory" is in-page only (no socket).
// Config.enableMutations: boolean â€” @default true. false â†’ only the 4 read-only tools are registered.
// Config.inMemoryGlobalKey: string â€” @default "__MOKU_GAME_MCP__" ("" disables the globalThis publish).
```

MCP tools â€” 15 total.
Read-only (always, 4): `ecs:query`, `renderer:screenshot`, `renderer:tree`, `scene:getInfo`.
Mutating / play (when `enableMutations: true`, 11): `ecs:spawn` (REAL components resolved via
`componentByName`), `ecs:despawn`, `ecs:setComponent`, `ecs:removeComponent`, `renderer:attach`
(visible primitive), `input:key`, `loop:step`, `loop:pause`, `loop:resume`, `scene:load`,
`game:reset`. Unknown component/scene names + dead entities return `isError`; despawn/remove report
`{ changed: boolean }`.
MCP resources: `game://world/snapshot` (every live entity + named values), `game://systems/list`
(stage names only â€” systems are anonymous), `game://stats/frame`, `game://scene/current`.

### app.audio (Standard) â€” native WebAudio SFX + music (zero deps)

```ts
unlock(): Promise<void>                          // resume AudioContext after a user gesture; idempotent; sets state.unlocked
load(name: string, url?: string): Promise<void>  // fetch + decodeAudioData + cache; url falls back to manifest[name]; one decode per name
play(name: string, opts?: { volume?: number; rate?: number }): void       // one-shot on sfx; no-op if headless/not-unlocked/not-loaded
playMusic(name: string, opts?: { loop?: boolean; fadeIn?: number }): void // looping on music; stops previous; fadeIn ramps from 0
stopMusic(opts?: { fadeOut?: number }): void     // stop current track; fadeOut ramps to 0 first
mute(): void                                     // zero the master mute bus; emits audio:muteChanged on change
unmute(): void                                   // restore master gain to masterVolume; emits on change
setMuted(muted: boolean): void                   // explicit; emits only on change
isMuted(): boolean
setVolume(channel: Channel, value: number): void // clamp 0..1; write gain; emit audio:volumeChanged on change; master-while-muted keeps bus at 0
getVolume(channel: Channel): number
// Channel = "master" | "sfx" | "music".
// Gain graph: source â†’ channelGain(sfx|music) â†’ masterGain(mute bus) â†’ destination. Held in a ctx.global WeakMap.
// Config: masterVolume/sfxVolume/musicVolume (0..1, @default 1), muted (@default false), manifest (nameâ†’url, @default {}).
//   Volume/mute config double as rehydration inputs a storage/platform plugin feeds via pluginConfigs.audio.*.
// Headless-safe: with no AudioContext (SSR/tests) every method no-ops (getters still return state). onStart/onStop own the AudioContext.
```

### app.storage (Standard) â€” versioned key/value save persistence (zero deps)

```ts
get<T>(key: string, fallback?: T): T | undefined  // JSON-parse; fallback on missing/unparseable/unavailable; triggers lazy migration; never throws
set(key: string, value: unknown): boolean         // JSON-serialize + write; false on rejected write / unserializable value; never throws
has(key: string): boolean
remove(key: string): void                          // no-op if absent
clear(): void                                      // wipe every namespace key, then re-stamp the schema version
keys(): string[]                                   // namespace keys, `${namespace}:` prefix stripped, meta key excluded; [] on failure
isPersistent(): boolean                            // false on the in-memory fallback / an injected non-persistent backend
getVersion(): number                               // schema version in effect after any lazy migration
setBackend(backend: StorageBackend): void          // inject platform's CrazyGames backend; resets `migrated` â†’ re-migrate on next access
// Values stored as `${namespace}:${key}` (JSON). StorageBackend = { getItemâ†’string|null, setItemâ†’boolean, removeItem, keys(prefix)â†’string[], persistent } â€” synchronous, non-throwing.
// Config: namespace (@default "game"), version (@default 1), migrations (target-version â†’ whole-namespace Snapshot transform, @default {}).
// Migration runs lazily on first access, once (memoized): migrations[n] upgrades a v(n-1) snapshot to vn; a store already at version is untouched; a downgrade warns + leaves data intact.
// Safe by construction: the default backend probes localStorage and falls back to an in-memory Map (persistent=false) when partitioned/blocked/absent â€” get/set never crash the game. No events, no hooks, no lifecycle.
```

### app.platform (Complex) â€” portal-SDK adapter layer (zero new deps)

```ts
getPortal(): Portal                              // "crazygames" | "poki" | "newgrounds" | "none" â€” resolved at onStart
gameplayStart(): void                            // signal active gameplay started (portals gate ad timing/analytics on it)
gameplayStop(): void                             // signal gameplay stopped (menu/pause/game-over)
loadingStart(): void                             // signal loading started (usually driven by onStart)
loadingFinished(): void                          // signal loading finished (game interactive)
commercialBreak(): Promise<void>                 // interstitial; pauses loop + mutes audio, restores on settle; frequency-capped; never rejects
rewardedAd(): Promise<boolean>                   // rewarded; same coordination; true = watched-to-completion (grant reward); noop adapter â†’ true
isAdPlaying(): boolean
// Portal = "crazygames" | "poki" | "newgrounds" | "none". Active adapter selected per build via ctx.env: config.portal "auto" â†’ ctx.env.get(portalEnvVar) (default GAME_PORTAL, case-insensitive); explicit config overrides; unknown/headless â†’ none.
// Ads are capture-then-restore: capture loop.isRunning()+audio.isMuted() before pausing+muting, restore only what changed on settle (resolve OR reject); re-entrancy guard (state.adPlaying); minInterstitialSeconds cap on interstitials. The same coordination drives window focus/blur + visibilitychange listeners.
// One PortalAdapter interface, four implementations in adapters/. CrazyGames provides a native StorageBackend over its async data API (synchronous, non-throwing facade over an init-hydrated write-through snapshot) injected via storage.setBackend() when useNativeStorage; Poki/Newgrounds keep storage's localStorage default.
// persistAudioPrefs (default): hooks audio:muteChanged/audio:volumeChanged â†’ storage.set("audio.muted"/"audio.volume.<channel>"); rehydrates audio mute/volume at onStart. Iframe-safe (own window, never window.top). onStart/onStop own the loaded SDK + focus/visibility listeners in a ctx.global WeakMap. No new package deps (SDKs runtime-injected, globals typed structurally, shipped .d.ts SDK-ambient-free).
// Config: portal (@default "auto"), portalEnvVar (@default "GAME_PORTAL"), pauseOnAd (@default true), minInterstitialSeconds (@default 60), useNativeStorage (@default true), persistAudioPrefs (@default true).
```

### app.vfx (Complex) â€” game-juice / visual-feedback layer (zero new deps)

```ts
createEmitter(spec: EmitterSpec): Entity        // persistent emitter entity (Emitter + Transform at spec.x/y); required: rate, speed, lifetime
configureEmitter(emitter, patch: Partial<EmitterSpec>): void  // shallow-merge emission params (position moves via the Transform, not here)
setEmitterEnabled(emitter, enabled): void       // pause/resume emission; existing particles stay alive
removeEmitter(emitter): void                    // despawn the emitter AND only its own live particles (burst particles untouched)
burst(x, y, spec: BurstSpec): void              // one-shot spark/explosion/pickup; required: count, speed, lifetime; defaults to full-circle spread
shake(amplitude, duration): void                // bank screen-shake trauma (0..1); render system offsets the stage root by traumaÂ² Â· shakeMaxOffset
stopShake(): void                               // clear trauma + reset the stage offset to (0,0) immediately
pop(entity, opts?): void                        // scale-pop a Transform to opts.scaleĂ— (@default 1.3) over opts.duration (@default 0.15s) and back; no-op without a Transform
flash(entity, opts?): void                      // hit-flash: snap view.tint to opts.color (@default white) then ease back to the captured base over opts.duration (@default 0.12s); alive entity, tint only if it has a view (headless â†’ ages out); re-call refreshes
floatText(x, y, text, opts?): Entity            // rising, alpha-fading floating number/text (e.g. "+50"); returns its entity handle
readonly easing: Readonly<Record<EasingName, (t: number) => number>>  // frozen curve table: linear, easeInQuad, easeOutQuad, easeInOutQuad, easeOutCubic, easeOutBack, easeOutElastic
lerp(a: number, b: number, t: number): number   // linear interpolation a + (b âˆ’ a) * t
// Particles are ECS entities (renderer.Transform + a named Particle) drawn via renderer.attachPrimitive â€” the renderer owns their whole view lifecycle; they fade by scaling out startScale â†’ endScale (no alpha), so NO new dependency (Pixi arrives via renderer). vfx keeps no per-particle handle; only floating-text Text handles are retained (to fade alpha).
// Emitter/Particle/Pop/Flash/FloatingText are named components (MCP-introspectable by name). A single maxParticles budget spans every emitter + burst; over-budget emission is dropped (ctx.log debug once per over-budget frame).
// Every effectful method is a guarded no-op before app.start() and on dead / wrong-type entities. Emits NO events (per-frame hot path â€” same stance as ecs/renderer/scheduler/loop). No onStop (the renderer disposes every view). Headless-safe: systems run identically; only Pixi view creation guards on renderer.getStage().
// Config: maxParticles (@default 1000), shakeDecay (@default 1.8), shakeMaxOffset (@default 24), defaultColor (@default 0xffffff).
```

### app.ui (Complex) â€” PixiJS-native game-UI layer (zero new deps)

```ts
pushScreen(spec: ScreenSpec): ScreenHandle      // build + push a screen (widgets + optional dimming backdrop); only the TOP screen's buttons are tappable (modal capture)
popScreen(): void                               // remove + destroy the top screen
replaceScreen(spec: ScreenSpec): ScreenHandle   // popScreen() then pushScreen(spec)
clearScreens(): void                            // remove + destroy every screen (HUD untouched)
topScreen(): ScreenHandle | undefined           // the top screen's handle, or undefined when empty
screenCount(): number                           // stack depth
addHud(spec: WidgetSpec): WidgetHandle          // persistent HUD widget (outside the stack); HUD buttons tappable only when the stack is empty
removeHud(handle): void                         // remove + destroy a HUD widget (and drop its buttons from the hit-set)
getWidget(screen: ScreenHandle, id: string): WidgetHandle | undefined  // resolve a screen widget by spec id (recursive through panels)
setText(handle, text): void                     // update a label/button caption (no-op on non-text / stale)
setValue(handle, value): void                   // update a bar (clamped to [0,max]; scales the fill)
setVisible(handle, visible): void               // show/hide a widget's node
getRoot(): Container | undefined                // the UI root Container (advanced), or undefined before start / headless
// Declarative plain-data specs: WidgetSpec = label | button | panel(children) | bar (discriminated on `kind`); ScreenSpec = { backdrop?, widgets }. ui builds the Pixi subtree once and returns opaque handles (retained-mode), parented under renderer.getStage(). NO Pixi types leak into specs; only the structural Container handle escapes via getRoot().
// A single "update"-stage system reads input.snapshot().pointer, computes its own press/release edges from the button bitmask, and fires a button's onTap on release over the SAME button armed on press. Hover fill re-paints as the pointer enters/leaves. Touch == mouse (both arrive as DOM pointer events). Modal capture: only the top screen (or the HUD when the stack is empty) is tappable.
// Emits NO events (interactions are declarative onTap callbacks). No onStop (the renderer disposes the whole subtree; in-run disposal is the API's job). Headless-safe: with renderer.getStage() undefined, no root is built and every method + the system are guarded no-ops.
// Config: textColor (@default 0xffffff), fontSize (@default 20), fontFamily (@default "sans-serif"), buttonColor (@default 0x3355ff), buttonHoverColor (@default 0x4466ff), panelColor (@default 0x141821), panelAlpha (@default 0.92), backdropColor (@default 0x000000), backdropAlpha (@default 0.6), padding (@default 12), width (@default 800), height (@default 600 â€” reference viewport for the backdrop/layout; the renderer exposes no dims via its API).
```

### app.tween (Standard) â€” shared ECS-agnostic tweening (zero new deps)

```ts
to<T>(target: T, props: NumericProperties<T>, opts?: TweenOptions): TweenHandle   // tween numeric props of any plain object TO the given values; start values captured at creation; non-finite props skipped
from<T>(target: T, props: NumericProperties<T>, opts?: TweenOptions): TweenHandle // apply the "from" values immediately, then tween back to the current values
value(from: number, to: number, opts: ValueTweenOptions): TweenHandle            // tween a bare scalar, driving opts.onUpdate(value) each frame (onUpdate required)
killAll(): void                                 // stop + drop every active tween (no onComplete; each done settles)
count(): number                                 // number of active tweens
easing: Readonly<Record<EasingName, (t) => number>>  // the frozen 7-curve table (parity with vfx; the canonical shared source)
lerp(a, b, t): number                           // linear interpolation a + (b âˆ’ a) * t
// TweenHandle: stop() (cancel now, no onComplete, settles done, idempotent) Â· pause()/resume() (freeze/unfreeze advancement) Â· active (still registered?) Â· done (Promise, settles on complete OR stop â€” never rejects/hangs).
// TweenOptions: duration (@default config.defaultDuration) Â· easing (EasingName | (t)=>number, @default config.defaultEasing) Â· delay (@default 0; does NOT defer start-value capture) Â· repeat (@default 0; Infinity allowed) Â· yoyo (@default false; ping-pong) Â· onUpdate(progress) Â· onComplete().
// One "update"-stage system advances all tweens by dt on the game clock, so a paused loop freezes every tween automatically (pause-safe, zero coupling to platform/loop). Advanced by scheduler ONLY (not ecs/renderer/loop). onStart-only wiring; NO onStop (tweens are plain GC-able data). Emits NO events. Headless-safe. Before-start / over-maxActive creators warn + return a dead handle (no hot-path throw).
// Config: defaultDuration (@default 0.3), defaultEasing (@default "easeOutCubic"), updateStage (@default "update"), maxActive (@default 2048).
```

### app.camera (Standard) â€” 2D follow/shake/parallax camera (consumes app.tween; zero new deps)

```ts
world: Container | undefined                    // the default world layer (parallax factor 1); undefined headless â€” add world content here
addLayer(name: string, factor: number): Container | undefined // create/return a named parallax layer (idempotent by name; factor NOT overwritten); undefined headless
layer(name: string): Container | undefined      // look up a previously-added layer container
follow(target?: { x: number; y: number }): void // continuously ease the centre toward a LIVE { x, y } each frame (a Container / entity Transform value / bare point); no arg clears
setPosition(x, y): void                         // snap the centre immediately (clears follow)
moveTo(x, y, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle   // animated pan via app.tween (clears follow); returns the tween handle
getPosition(): Point                            // the current centre (a fresh copy â€” never the mutable state object)
setZoom(zoom): void                             // set zoom immediately, clamped to [minZoom, maxZoom]
zoomTo(zoom, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle   // animated zoom via app.tween (final value clamped)
getZoom(): number
setRotation(radians): void                      // set rotation immediately
rotateTo(radians, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle // animated rotate via app.tween
getRotation(): number
shake(intensity, duration, opts?: ShakeOptions): void // additive screen shake decaying to 0 over duration s via app.tween; replaces any in-flight shake
screenToWorld(point: Point): Point              // map a screen point to world space (picking); pure, works before start
worldToScreen(point: Point): Point              // map a world point to screen space (place UI over a world object); pure
// MoveOptions = Pick<TweenOptions, "duration"|"easing"|"delay"|"onComplete"|"onUpdate"> (NO repeat/yoyo â€” a repeating pan is nonsensical). ShakeOptions = { easing? } (decay curve, @default "linear").
// Transforms world-space LAYER containers it OWNS (world at stage index 0 via addChildAt, below the ui overlay), NEVER the root stage â€” so the HUD stays screen-fixed and parallax works (one plane per layer). Limitation: renderer.attachPrimitive self-parents to the stage root â†’ NOT camera-transformed; the camera path is renderer.attach + camera.world/a layer.
// One "sync"-stage system eases centreâ†’target (via app.tween.lerp), writes each layer's pivot/position/scale/rotation, and adds the shake offset. moveTo/zoomTo/rotateTo/shake + follow-lerp all delegate to app.tween â†’ pause-safe for free (rides scheduler.tick; NO platform/loop edge). Depends on renderer + scheduler + tween (all real edges); NOT ecs (structural {x,y} target) / NOT loop. onStart-only (@no-resource-check); NO onStop (containers parented under the renderer-owned stage; no external resource). Emits NO events. Headless-safe (numeric state + screenâ†”world still track).
// Config: zoom (@default 1), minZoom (@default 0.1), maxZoom (@default 10), followLerp (@default 0.15), width (@default 800), height (@default 600), updateStage (@default "sync"). width/height = reference viewport (the renderer exposes no canvas dims via API â€” the ui-plugin precedent).
reset(): void                                   // EDITOR DELTA: recentre (0,0), zoomâ†’config.zoom, rotationâ†’0, clear follow, stop shake â€” editor-runtime.stop() calls this on exit-play
```

### Editor subsystem (Layer-2, additive â€” plugins #17â€“#24)

An in-browser Unity-style editor over the live ECS. Single write-authority, poll-on-epoch reactivity (no per-frame emit), two-`createApp` split. **Pay-for-what-you-use:** a non-editor game never calls these; `editor-runtime` does not gate the scheduler until `enterEdit()`. The extension deltas that support it: `ecs` gains `changeEpoch()` (per-write counter) + `setActiveStages`/`activeStages` (stage gate); `scheduler` forwards `setActiveStages`/`activeStages`; `assets` gains `entries()`/`manifest()`/`metadata()`; `tween`/`vfx`/`camera` gain `reset()`.

```ts
// app.commands (Standard) â€” the SINGLE validated write-authority; owns the save-durable branded EditorId. Depends on ecs only.
apply(command: Command): CommandResult          // validate + apply, returns the inverse ({ ok:true; inverse } | { ok:false; error })
applyRaw(command: Command): RawResult           // the primitive editor-history wraps (no inverse)
restore(entities: readonly RestoreEntity[], source: "reload"|"exit-play"): void // non-undoable bulk reseed; emits commands:restored
resolve(id: EditorId): Entity | undefined       // validate-before-return against world.isAlive (prunes stale); editorIdOf(entity): EditorId | undefined; count(): number
setValidator(fn: FieldValidator | undefined): void // inject reflection.validate (the decoupling seam)
// Command = discriminated union: spawn | despawn | setField | addComponent | removeComponent (components by NAME, plain data)

// app.reflection (Standard) â€” field-schema registry for named components. Depends on ecs only.
describe(componentName: string): FieldDescriptor[] // registered schema, else inferred from a live value by typeof, else []
register(componentName: string, schema: Schema): void // typed field.* schema (registered wins over inference)
validate(componentName: string, partial): ValidationResult // pure check used by commands.setValidator
field: FieldBuilders                            // number/boolean/string/color/select/vector2/readonly builders (also re-exported standalone)

// app.serialization (Complex) â€” scene (de)serializer. Depends on ecs/storage/commands/reflection.
serialize(): SceneDocument                      // walk liveEntities, capture editor-owned entities' named components, keyed by EditorId
deserialize(doc): void                          // migrate (via storage chain) â†’ validate (reflection) â†’ commands.restore atomically â†’ emit serialization:loaded
save(name)/load(name): boolean; list(): string[]; export(): string; import(json): void

// app["editor-selection"] (Standard) â€” viewport picking + selection. Depends on ecs/renderer/camera/input.
enable()/disable(): void                        // flip Pixi eventMode on ONE camera pick layer (zero cost outside edit mode)
pickAt(screen: Point): Entity | undefined       // topmost entity via a non-enumerable stamped handle; select/toggle/clear; selected(): readonly Entity[]; isSelected(e): boolean
// emits editor-selection:changed only on a real change; headless-safe

// app["editor-history"] (Standard) â€” undo/redo authority. Depends on commands.
applyTracked(command): CommandResult            // wraps commands.applyRaw synchronously + records a bounded field-diff inverse
undo()/redo(): boolean; canUndo()/canRedo(): boolean // poll on ecs changeEpoch
beginGesture()/endGesture(): void               // coalesce a drag burst into ONE undo entry; clear(): void (also on commands:restored, via hooks)

// app["editor-gizmos"] (Complex) â€” direct-manipulation translate gizmo (overlay outside the ECS). Depends on renderer/camera/editor-selection/commands.
enable()/disable(): void; setMode(mode)/mode(): translate only in MVP (rotate/scale warn+no-op); setSnap(n): void
setGestureSink(sink | undefined): void          // injected editor-history {begin,applyTracked,end}; drag deltas recomputed via camera.screenToWorld EVERY event (anti-drift), commits â‰¤2 setField on pointerup

// app["editor-runtime"] (Complex) â€” edit/play FSM via scheduler stage-gating. Depends on loop/scheduler/serialization/commands/tween/vfx/camera.
enterEdit(): void                               // gate to config.editStages (update/physics OFF); does NOT gate at boot (pay-for-what-you-use)
enterPlay(): void                               // snapshot (serialize) + un-gate (setActiveStages undefined) + loop.start
stop(): void                                    // restore snapshot (commands.restore) THEN tween/vfx/camera reset() THEN re-gate; step(): TimeStepResult; mode()/isPlaying()
// emits editor-runtime:modeChanged on each flip

// app["editor-bridge"] (Complex) â€” the terminal facade (Layer-3 web editor's single seam). Depends on all 9 editor seams + mcp; registered LAST.
snapshot(): EditorSnapshot                      // immutable, poll-on-epoch (epoch mirrors world.changeEpoch; entities memoized by epoch; selection/mode/canUndo/canRedo read fresh)
apply(command)/setField(id, component, field, value): CommandResult // â†’ editor-history.applyTracked â†’ commands.applyRaw (undo-tracked)
select/clearSelection; undo/redo; play/stop/step; save/load; describe(component) // forward to the owning plugin
// onStart wires commands.setValidator(reflection.validate); probes mcp readiness (mirror is fast-follow). Emits nothing; no hooks; headless-safe.
```

## Events

The catalog is intentionally tiny â€” per-frame work is on the hot path, off the event bus.

- `assets:loaded` â€” `{ alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" }` â€” after `load()`/`loadBundle()`. (framework Events)
- `scene:loaded` â€” `{ name: string }` â€” after a scene's `setup` completes during `load()`. (framework Events)
- `game:reset` â€” `{ reason: "mcp" }` â€” after the mcp `game:reset` tool despawns all MCP-tracked
  entities and unloads the current scene. (mcp plugin event â€” declared + emitted by the mcp plugin)
- `audio:muteChanged` â€” `{ muted: boolean }` â€” after `mute()`/`unmute()`/`setMuted()` changes the
  mute state. (audio plugin event â€” a storage/platform plugin persists it)
- `audio:volumeChanged` â€” `{ channel: "master" | "sfx" | "music"; value: number }` â€” after
  `setVolume()` changes a channel volume. (audio plugin event)
- `platform:ready` â€” `{ portal: Portal }` â€” once the active portal adapter is initialised and loading
  is signalled finished. (platform plugin event)
- `platform:adStart` â€” `{ type: "interstitial" | "rewarded" }` â€” when an ad begins, after `loop` is
  paused + `audio` muted. (platform plugin event)
- `platform:adEnd` â€” `{ type: "interstitial" | "rewarded"; rewarded?: boolean }` â€” when an ad ends,
  after `loop` + `audio` are restored; `rewarded` set for rewarded ads. (platform plugin event)
- `commands:restored` â€” `{ source: "reload" | "exit-play" }` â€” after a non-undoable `commands.restore()`
  reseeds the world + EditorId map; editor-history listens + clears its stacks. (commands plugin event)
- `serialization:loaded` â€” `{ name: string; entityCount: number }` â€” after `deserialize()`/`load()` and
  the named scene is live (distinct from the lower-level commands:restored). (serialization plugin event)
- `editor-selection:changed` â€” `{ selected: readonly Entity[] }` â€” after the editor selection set changes
  (user-gesture frequency, never per-frame). (editor-selection plugin event)
- `editor-runtime:modeChanged` â€” `{ mode: "edit" | "play" }` â€” after an editâ†”play flip. (editor-runtime plugin event)

`assets:loaded`/`scene:loaded` are declared in `src/config.ts` (framework `Events`); `game:reset`
(mcp), `audio:muteChanged`/`audio:volumeChanged` (audio), `platform:ready`/`platform:adStart`/`platform:adEnd`
(platform), and the editor milestones `commands:restored`/`serialization:loaded`/`editor-selection:changed`/`editor-runtime:modeChanged`
are declared on their plugins' `Events`. The editor subsystem is otherwise POLL-ON-EPOCH â€” panels read `app["editor-bridge"].snapshot()` on their own tick.
Subscribe from a consumer plugin's `hooks`. The `context` plugin exposes the framework emitter to
systems via `GameContext.emit`, but owns no events of its own.

## File Structure

```
game/
â”œâ”€â”€ src/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ config.ts            # Layer 1: createCoreConfig, Config = {}, Events, createPlugin/createCore
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts             # Layer 2: createCore assembles the 24 plugins (16 runtime + 8 editor); exports createApp/createPlugin (+ createCore/createCoreConfig for advanced/headless assembly)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ plugins/
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts         # plugin barrel (instances + type namespaces)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ ecs/             # data/runtime core + introspection + resource registry (world.ts, types.ts, README.md, __tests__/)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ scheduler/       # stage contract facade
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ renderer/        # PixiJS v8 backend (api.ts, lifecycle.ts, sync.ts, primitives.ts, types.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ input/           # polled keyboard/pointer + key injection (api.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ loop/            # fixed-timestep rAF loop + Time resource (resources.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ assets/          # Pixi Assets wrapper
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ context/         # binds Assets + GameContext world resources (resources.ts, lifecycle.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ scene/           # named scene lifecycle + ownership
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ mcp/             # MCP server (transport.ts, tools.ts, resources.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ audio/           # native WebAudio SFX + music, mute bus (engine.ts, lifecycle.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ storage/         # versioned key/value save persistence (backend.ts, migrate.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ platform/        # portal-SDK adapter layer (api.ts, lifecycle.ts, prefs.ts, adapters/{crazygames,poki,newgrounds,noop})
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ vfx/             # game-juice / visual-feedback layer (api.ts, lifecycle.ts, easing.ts, views.ts, state.ts, systems/{emit,particles,shake,pop,floating})
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ ui/              # PixiJS-native UI: screen stack + widgets + HUD + pointer hit-testing (widgets.ts, system.ts, api.ts, lifecycle.ts, state.ts)
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ tween/           # shared ECS-agnostic tweening advanced by one scheduler system (api.ts, advance.ts, easing.ts, state.ts)
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ camera/          # 2D follow/shake/parallax camera over world-space layer containers; delegates animated moves + follow-lerp to app.tween (api.ts, apply.ts, transform.ts, state.ts)
â”œâ”€â”€ tests/                   # framework-level unit + integration tests
â”œâ”€â”€ .planning/specs/         # design specs (intent)
â”œâ”€â”€ README.md
â”œâ”€â”€ llms.txt
â””â”€â”€ llms-full.txt
```

## Plugins

Built/resolved in dependency order. (depends â†’ resolved with `ctx.require`.)

- **ecs** â€” data/runtime core: generational entities, archetype object-SoA storage, typed queries,
  deferred command buffer, read-only introspection facet, typed world resource registry,
  `world.tick`. | Complex | `defineComponent`/`spawn`/`query`/`addSystem`/`liveEntities`/
  `entityCount`/`componentNames`/`componentsOf`/`componentByName`/`defineResource`/`setResource`/
  `getResource`/`resource`/`hasResource`/`removeResource`/`tick` | events: none | depends: none.
- **scheduler** â€” ordered stage contract; thin facade over the world. | Standard |
  `addSystem(stage,fn)`/`tick`/`stages` | events: none | depends: ecs.
- **renderer** â€” PixiJS v8 backend; owns the GPU `Application`, defines `Transform`, syncs
  ECSâ†’display objects on the `sync` stage, attaches plain-data primitives. First-class `headless`
  config (auto-detected; skips Pixi/GPU, still defines `Transform` + sync). | Complex |
  `Transform`/`attach`/`attachPrimitive`/`detach`/`render`/`screenshot`/`tree`/`getView`/`getStage`/
  `getEntityView`/`markDirty` | new types: `PrimitiveSpec`, `PrimitiveStyle`, `SceneNode` | config: +`headless` |
  events: none | depends: ecs, scheduler.
- **input** â€” polled keyboard/pointer from DOM listeners â†’ per-frame `InputSnapshot` on the `input`
  stage; programmatic `keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` injection with alias normalization
  (`"Space"`/`"Spacebar"`â†’`" "`, `"Esc"`â†’`"Escape"`). | Standard |
  `snapshot`/`keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` | events: none | depends: scheduler.
- **loop** â€” fixed-timestep rAF loop driving `scheduler.tick` then `renderer.render` each frame
  (kernel-bypassing hot path); publishes + mutates the `Time` world resource in place each step;
  `step()` returns a `TimeStepResult` clock snapshot. | Standard | `start`/`stop`/`step`/
  `isRunning`/`time` | new type: `TimeStepResult` | events: none | depends: scheduler, renderer, ecs.
- **assets** â€” Pixi v8 `Assets` wrapper: load/cache textures + bundles, build sprites. | Standard |
  `load`/`loadBundle`/`sprite`/`get`/`isLoaded` | events: `assets:loaded` | depends: renderer.
- **context** â€” binds the well-known `Assets` + `GameContext` world resources at start so systems
  reach framework services via `world.resource(token)`; static API exposes the two tokens. |
  Standard | `app.context` â†’ `assets`/`game` | events: none | depends: ecs, assets.
- **scene** â€” named scene lifecycle with entity-ownership tracking, clean transitions, bundle
  pre-load. | Standard | `define`/`load`/`unload`/`currentScene`/`sceneNames`/`ownedEntities` |
  events: `scene:loaded` | depends: ecs, renderer, assets.
- **mcp** â€” first-class MCP server exposing the runtime to agents over stdio / Streamable HTTP /
  in-page `inMemory` (env-aware default: `["stdio"]` Node-Bun, `["inMemory"]` browser; stdio guarded
  on `process?.stdin`). 15 tools (4 read-only; 11 mutation/play gated by `enableMutations`). |
  Complex | `isRunning`/`httpEndpoint`/`toolNames`/`clientTransport` | config: +`inMemoryGlobalKey` |
  events: `game:reset` (`{ reason: "mcp" }`) | depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, assets, scene.
- **audio** â€” native WebAudio SFX + music (zero runtime deps, no Howler/Pixi). Owns a small gain
  graph `source â†’ channelGain(sfx|music) â†’ masterGain â†’ destination` whose `masterGain` is the
  **mute bus** the `platform` adapter ducks on ad breaks. Pooled one-shots (decoded-buffer
  cache), looping music with fade, per-channel + master volume, user-gesture `unlock()` (no
  autoplay). Headless-safe (every method no-ops without an `AudioContext`); owns the `AudioContext`
  via onStart/onStop. | Standard | `unlock`/`load`/`play`/`playMusic`/`stopMusic`/`mute`/`unmute`/
  `setMuted`/`isMuted`/`setVolume`/`getVolume` | new types: `Channel`, `PlayOptions`,
  `PlayMusicOptions`, `StopMusicOptions` | events: `audio:muteChanged`, `audio:volumeChanged` |
  depends: none (foundational, Wave 1).
- **storage** â€” namespaced, versioned key/value save persistence with a migration chain behind a
  pluggable synchronous `StorageBackend` seam. Safe default: probes `localStorage`, falls back to an
  in-memory `Map` (partitioned/blocked/absent) â€” get/set never throw. Migration runs lazily on first
  access, once (memoized); `setBackend()` (the `platform` CrazyGames adapter) resets it. No
  events, no hooks, no lifecycle. | Standard | `get`/`set`/`has`/`remove`/`clear`/`keys`/
  `isPersistent`/`getVersion`/`setBackend` | new types: `StorageBackend`, `Migration`, `Snapshot` |
  config: `namespace`/`version`/`migrations` | events: none | depends: none (foundational, Wave 1).
- **platform** â€” portal-SDK adapter layer (CrazyGames / Poki / Newgrounds / no-op) selected per build
  via `ctx.env` (`config.portal "auto"` â†’ `ctx.env.get(portalEnvVar)`; explicit config overrides;
  unknown/headless â†’ `none`). Promise-based ads that **capture-then-restore-pause** `loop` + mute
  `audio` on settle (frequency-capped interstitials, re-entrancy guard, never reject); one
  `PortalAdapter` interface + four `adapters/` implementations. Injects a portal-native
  `StorageBackend` into `storage` via `setBackend()` (CrazyGames async data API bridged behind a sync
  facade; Poki/Newgrounds keep localStorage). Persists + rehydrates `audio` mute/volume through
  `storage` (hooks `audio:muteChanged`/`audio:volumeChanged`). Iframe-safe; owns the loaded SDK +
  focus/visibility listeners via onStart/onStop (`ctx.global` WeakMap). No new package deps. | Complex |
  `getPortal`/`gameplayStart`/`gameplayStop`/`loadingStart`/`loadingFinished`/`commercialBreak`/
  `rewardedAd`/`isAdPlaying` | new types: `Portal`, `AdType`, `PortalAdapter`, `AdapterContext` |
  config: `portal`/`portalEnvVar`/`pauseOnAd`/`minInterstitialSeconds`/`useNativeStorage`/`persistAudioPrefs` |
  events: `platform:ready`, `platform:adStart`, `platform:adEnd` | depends: audio, loop, storage (Wave 2).
- **vfx** â€” the game-juice / visual-feedback layer. ECS-native particle emitters + one-shot bursts
  (each particle is an entity with `renderer.Transform` + a named `Particle`, drawn via
  `renderer.attachPrimitive` so the renderer owns the view lifecycle; particles fade by scaling out
  `startScale â†’ endScale`), trauma-based screen shake (offsets the Pixi stage root by
  `traumaÂ² Â· shakeMaxOffset`, decays by `shakeDecay`), Transform scale-pop, hit-flash tint (snaps a
  view's `tint` to a color then eases it back via `renderer.getEntityView`), floating rising/alpha-fading
  damage/score `Text`, and pure easing curves + `lerp` (vfx ships its own identical table; a `tween`
  dedupe via depends+ctx.require is deferred). `onStart` captures `renderer.Transform`, defines five named components
  (`Emitter`/`Particle`/`Pop`/`Flash`/`FloatingText`), and registers six systems
  (emit/particle/pop/flash/floating in `"update"`, shake in `"render"`); no `onStop` (the renderer disposes
  every view). A single `maxParticles` budget spans every emitter + burst. Headless-safe (systems run;
  only Pixi view creation guards on `renderer.getStage()`). No new package dep (Pixi via `renderer`). |
  Complex | `createEmitter`/`configureEmitter`/`setEmitterEnabled`/`removeEmitter`/`burst`/`shake`/
  `stopShake`/`pop`/`flash`/`floatText`/`easing`/`lerp` | new types: `EmitterSpec`, `BurstSpec`, `PopOptions`,
  `FlashOptions`, `FloatTextOptions`, `EasingName` | config: `maxParticles`/`shakeDecay`/`shakeMaxOffset`/`defaultColor` |
  events: none | depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer (Wave 3; registered after platform, before mcp).
- **ui** â€” the PixiJS-native game-UI layer. A screen stack (`pushScreen`/`popScreen`/`replaceScreen`/
  `clearScreens`/`topScreen`/`screenCount`) of modal cards (title / pause / game-over / upgrade); a
  declarative widget set (`label`/`button`/`panel`/`bar`) described as plain data and built once into a
  Pixi subtree under `renderer.getStage()` (retained-mode, opaque handles); a persistent HUD
  (`addHud`/`removeHud`) mutated via `setText`/`setValue`/`setVisible`, resolved by `getWidget`; and a
  single `"update"`-stage pointer/touch hit-test system that computes its own press/release edges from
  `input.snapshot().pointer` and fires a button's `onTap` on release over the same armed button (modal
  capture: only the top screen â€” or the HUD when the stack is empty â€” is tappable). `onStart` captures
  `renderer.getStage()`, builds the root, and registers the system; no `onStop` (the renderer disposes
  the whole subtree; in-run disposal is the API's job). Headless-safe (no stage â†’ no root; every method +
  the system are no-ops). No new package dep (Pixi via `renderer`). | Complex |
  `pushScreen`/`popScreen`/`replaceScreen`/`clearScreens`/`topScreen`/`screenCount`/`addHud`/`removeHud`/
  `getWidget`/`setText`/`setValue`/`setVisible`/`getRoot` | new types: `ScreenSpec`, `WidgetSpec`
  (`LabelSpec`/`ButtonSpec`/`PanelSpec`/`BarSpec`), `ScreenHandle`, `WidgetHandle` | config:
  `textColor`/`fontSize`/`fontFamily`/`buttonColor`/`buttonHoverColor`/`panelColor`/`panelAlpha`/
  `backdropColor`/`backdropAlpha`/`padding`/`width`/`height` | events: none | depends: renderer,
  scheduler, input (registered after vfx, before mcp).
- **tween** â€” shared, ECS-agnostic tweening. `to`/`from` mutate the numeric props of any plain object
  over time; `value` drives a scalar `onUpdate`; each tween supports `duration`/`easing` (named curve or
  custom `(t)=>number`)/`delay`/`repeat`/`yoyo` and returns an opaque `TweenHandle`
  (`stop`/`pause`/`resume`/`active` + a `done` Promise). Re-exposes the canonical 7-curve `easing` table
  + `lerp`. One `"update"`-stage system advances all tweens by `dt` on the game clock, so a paused loop
  freezes every tween for free (pause-safe, zero coupling to platform/loop). | Standard |
  `to`/`from`/`value`/`killAll`/`count`/`easing`/`lerp` | new types: `TweenOptions`, `ValueTweenOptions`,
  `TweenHandle`, `Easing`, `EasingName`, `NumericProperties` | config: `defaultDuration`/`defaultEasing`/
  `updateStage`/`maxActive` | events: none | onStart-only (no onStop) | depends: scheduler only
  (registered after ui, before mcp).
- **camera** â€” a 2D game camera. `follow(target?)` continuously eases the centre toward a LIVE `{ x, y }`
  (a Container / entity Transform value / bare point â€” no `ecs` edge); instant `setPosition`/`setZoom`/
  `setRotation` and animated `moveTo`/`zoomTo`/`rotateTo` (delegate to `app.tween`, return a `TweenHandle`);
  decaying `shake(intensity, duration)` (drives magnitudeâ†’0 via `app.tween.value`); parallax via
  `world`/`addLayer(name, factor)`/`layer(name)`; and `screenToWorld`/`worldToScreen` mapping. Transforms
  world-space LAYER containers it OWNS (world at stage index 0, below the ui overlay), NEVER the root stage
  â€” so the HUD stays screen-fixed and parallax works. One `"sync"`-stage system eases centreâ†’target (via
  `app.tween.lerp`) and writes each layer's pivot/position/scale/rotation + shake offset. Pause-safe (rides
  `scheduler.tick`) and headless-safe (numeric state + screenâ†”world still track). | Standard |
  `follow`/`setPosition`/`moveTo`/`getPosition`/`setZoom`/`zoomTo`/`getZoom`/`setRotation`/`rotateTo`/
  `getRotation`/`shake`/`world`/`addLayer`/`layer`/`screenToWorld`/`worldToScreen` | new types: `Layer`,
  `FollowTarget`, `Point`, `MoveOptions`, `ShakeOptions` | config: `zoom`/`minZoom`/`maxZoom`/`followLerp`/
  `width`/`height`/`updateStage` | events: none | onStart-only (no onStop) | depends: renderer + scheduler
  + tween (registered after tween, before mcp). EDITOR DELTA: gains `reset()` (recentre/zoom/rotation/follow/shake).

**Editor subsystem (Layer-2, additive â€” #17â€“#24, registered after `mcp`).** In-browser Unity-style editor over the live ECS; single write-authority, poll-on-epoch reactivity, pay-for-what-you-use (a non-editor game pays nothing). Supported by extension deltas: `ecs` gains `changeEpoch()` + `setActiveStages`/`activeStages`; `scheduler` forwards `setActiveStages`/`activeStages`; `assets` gains `entries`/`manifest`/`metadata`; `tween`/`vfx` gain `reset()`.
- **commands** â€” the SINGLE validated write-authority; owns the branded save-durable `EditorId`. | Standard | `apply`/`applyRaw`/`restore`/`resolve`/`editorIdOf`/`setValidator`/`count` | events: `commands:restored` | depends: ecs.
- **reflection** â€” field-schema registry for named components (infer or typed `field.*` schema; `validate`). | Standard | `describe`/`register`/`validate`/`field` | events: none | depends: ecs.
- **serialization** â€” scene (de)serializer keyed by `EditorId`; migrates via `storage`, validates via `reflection`, routes loads through `commands.restore`. | Complex | `serialize`/`deserialize`/`save`/`load`/`list`/`export`/`import` | events: `serialization:loaded` | depends: ecs, storage, commands, reflection.
- **editor-selection** â€” viewport picking + selection (`enable`/`disable` flip Pixi eventMode on one camera layer; `pickAt` via a stamped handle). | Standard | `enable`/`disable`/`select`/`toggle`/`clear`/`selected`/`isSelected`/`pickAt` | events: `editor-selection:changed` | depends: ecs, renderer, camera, input.
- **editor-history** â€” undo/redo authority (`applyTracked` wraps `commands.applyRaw`; gesture coalescing; ring buffer; clears on `commands:restored`). | Standard | `applyTracked`/`undo`/`redo`/`canUndo`/`canRedo`/`beginGesture`/`endGesture`/`clear` | events: none | depends: commands.
- **editor-gizmos** â€” direct-manipulation translate gizmo (overlay outside the ECS; anti-drift via `camera.screenToWorld` per event; commits via `commands`, coalesced via an injected sink). | Complex | `enable`/`disable`/`setMode`/`setSnap`/`mode`/`setGestureSink` | events: none | depends: renderer, camera, editor-selection, commands.
- **editor-runtime** â€” edit/play FSM via scheduler stage-gating; `stop()` restores the snapshot then `reset()`s tween/vfx/camera. Does NOT gate at boot (pay-for-what-you-use). | Complex | `enterEdit`/`enterPlay`/`stop`/`step`/`mode`/`isPlaying` | events: `editor-runtime:modeChanged` | depends: loop, scheduler, serialization, commands, tween, vfx, camera.
- **editor-bridge** â€” the terminal facade (Layer-3 web editor's single seam); `snapshot()` poll-on-epoch; forwards writes through the authority; wires `commands.setValidator(reflection.validate)` at onStart. Registered LAST. | Complex | `snapshot`/`apply`/`setField`/`select`/`clearSelection`/`undo`/`redo`/`play`/`stop`/`step`/`save`/`load`/`describe` | events: none | depends: ecs, reflection, commands, editor-selection, editor-gizmos, editor-history, editor-runtime, serialization, mcp.

## Requirements

- Node >= 24, Bun >= 1.3.14 (use bun exclusively). TypeScript strict +
  `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- Dependencies: `@moku-labs/core`, `@moku-labs/common`, `pixi.js@^8`. Composable with
  `@moku-labs/web`. mcp adds `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono`, `zod`.

## Scripts

`bun run build` (tsdown) Â· `bun run lint` (biome + eslint) Â· `bun run lint:fix` Â·
`bun run format` Â· `bun run test` (vitest run) Â· `bun run test:unit` Â·
`bun run test:integration` Â· `bun run test:coverage`.
